holeInOne.firstRow: We expected all the letters to be green

entirelyWrong.firstRow: We expected the first row to be absent
entirelyWrong.secondRow: We expected the second row to be correct

twoNeeded.firstRow: We expected the first row to have one correct
twoNeeded.secondRow: We expected the second row to be correct

threeNeeded.firstRow: We expected the first row to have one correct
threeNeeded.secondRow: We expected the second row to have all but one correct
threeNeeded.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be correct

fourNeeded.firstRow: We expected the first row to have one correct
fourNeeded.secondRow: We expected the second row to have the first two correct
fourNeeded.thirdRow: We expected the third row have four correct
fourNeeded.fourthRow: We expected the fourth row to be correct

present1.firstRow: We expected the first row to be present, absent, correct, correct, absent.
present1.secondRow: We expected the second row to be all correct

present2.firstRow: We expected the first row to be present, absent, absent, absent, absent
present2.secondRow: We expected the second row to be absent, present, absent, present, present
present2.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be correct

present3.firstRow: We expected the first row to be absent, absent, present, present, absent.
present3.secondRow: We expected the second row to be absent, absent, absent, correct, correct
present3.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be correct, absent, absent, correct, correct
present3.fourthRow: We expected the fourth row to be correct

present4.firstRow: We expected the first row to be absent
present4.secondRow: We expected the second row to be absent
present4.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be present, absent, present, absent, absent
present4.fourthRow: We expected the fourth row to be correct, correct, correct, present, present
present4.fifthRow: We expected the fifth row to be correct

bonus1.firstRow: We expected only the fourth letter of the first row to be present.
bonus1.secondRow: We expected the second row to be correct, absent, present, absent, absent (This is the tricky one!)
bonus1.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be correct, correct, correct, absent, absent
bonus1.fourthRow: We expected the fourth row to be correct

bonus2.firstRow: We expected only the first row to be present, absent, correct, absent, absent.
bonus2.secondRow: We expected the second row to be correct, correct, correct, absent, absent
bonus2.thirdRow: We expected the third row to be correct, correct, correct, absent, absent.
bonus2.fourthRow: We expected the fourth row to be correct, correct, correct, absent, correct (this is the tricky one!)
bonus2.fifthRow: We expected the fifth row to be correct, correct, correct, absent, correct.
bonus2.sixthRow: We expected the sixth row to be correct.

firstWord.name: Get a correct word displaying
firstWord.description: Start by guessing the first word from commonWords(). If it matches, add it with all correct states.

handleWrong.name: Handle entirely wrong
handleWrong.description: When a guess is entirely wrong, filter it out and try the next word.

handlePartial.name: Handle partially right
handlePartial.description: Use correct letter positions to narrow down guesses.

handlePresent.name: Handle present letters
handlePresent.description: Use present (yellow) letters to narrow down guesses further.

bonus.name: Bonus: Handle duplicate letters
bonus.description: If a letter appears twice in a guess but only once in the target, only one should be yellow.

holeInOne.name: All correct
holeInOne.description: Deal with a first correct guess.

present1.name: Some letters present
present1.description: A tough one with some letters present.

present4.name: Another close one
present4.description: Its fun how they diverge at the end!

bonus1.name: Only one yellow
bonus1.description: If there are multiple present letters for only one actual letter, only one should be yellow.

bonus2.name: Swiss Cheese
bonus2.description: The swiss in this shows why it's important!

whereToStart.question: Where do I even start?
whereToStart.answer: Begin by always guessing the first word from `commonWords()`. Get the loop that adds a guess and checks the target working before worrying about being smart.

generateStates.question: How do I generate the states for each guess?
generateStates.answer: Compare the guess letter-by-letter to `getTargetWord()`, exactly like in the previous Wordle exercises: `"correct"`, `"present"`, or `"absent"`.

rememberLearned.question: How do I remember what I've learned across guesses?
rememberLearned.answer: Keep a knowledge structure outside the loop: a list of letters known to be absent, a list of `(letter, position)` pairs that were correct, and per-position lists of letters known NOT to be there.

pickNextGuess.question: How do I pick the next guess?
pickNextGuess.answer: Filter `commonWords()`: keep only words that have all the correct letters in the right slots, contain every present letter (but not in positions you've ruled out), and contain none of the absent letters. Pick the first match.

whenToStop.question: When do I stop?
whenToStop.answer: Stop as soon as the guess equals the target word, or after `6` guesses. Otherwise you'll keep guessing past the end of the board.

addWord.description: Adds a word to the Wordle board at the given row (1-6) with its letter states.

getTargetWord.description: Returns the secret target word. Use this to check your guess, not to cheat!

commonWords.description: Returns a list of 100+ common five-letter words to use as guesses.

push.description: Adds an element to a list and returns the new list. Does not change the original list.

describers.addWord: added a word to the wordle board
describers.commonWords: retrieved the list of common words
